headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release drove the quarter's central story, pairing a new Mythos-class model with invisible capability restrictions on frontier AI work.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5, its first publicly available Mythos-class model spanning software engineering, vision, analytics, and cybersecurity, with safety systems that cap high-risk domains [8]. Reporting detailed silent interventions that degrade performance on roughly 0.03% of traffic tied to pretraining pipelines and accelerator design, using prompt modification and steering vectors rather than refusals [10], while comparisons placed Opus 4.8 ahead of Fable 5.0 on agentic coding tasks [9]. Accountability pressure rose as a German court held Google liable for false AI Overviews answers and lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated fake citations [4][7]. Meanwhile the efficiency and orchestration techniques that led prior windows, design system, agent orchestration, context extension, and token efficiency, all weakened, as scaling laws, attribution, and CPU offloading broke out.

cited_items.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
Fable 5.0 vs Opus 4.8: AI Model Powerhouse Comparison! #shorts
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
